[2018-06-02 19:13] apooche: Gaussite pivot seems like a crazy suggestion to me. To many critical units like antima and Wincer only cost one green
[2018-06-02 19:13] darkeisbein: is there something that can be done about opponents that just let their turn timer run out 4 turns in a row when they loose?
[2018-06-02 19:14] cachotognax: play faster modes sadly, that is sone BS behaviour.
[2018-06-02 19:14] masn6811 (Masne98888): ugh, antima is a problem, but giving a blue or a red is a bigger issue
[2018-06-02 19:15] Deleted User (Deleted User): what is goosite pivot
[2018-06-02 19:15] masn6811 (Masne98888): other ideas: B->G, 1G->GG, but they aren't as elegant
[2018-06-02 19:16] masn6811 (Masne98888): :Click: , 1 :Gold: : Gain :Green: . Only player 1 may use this ability, only once per game, and only on turn 5 or later.
[2018-06-02 19:16] masn6811 (Masne98888): well, i feel like antima isn't that big a problem actually
[2018-06-02 19:16] masn6811 (Masne98888): with chrono, p2 can set it up on turn 4, so it's not an issue
[2018-06-02 19:16] Deleted User (Deleted User): what about if it was 2 :Gold:
[2018-06-02 19:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): and antima isn't especially busted otherwise, and besides, it would be onyl saving a conduit
[2018-06-02 19:17] apooche: I just think giving one side access to 1 gold for a color resource is going to create a lot of problems
[2018-06-02 19:17] apooche: The point of this presumably is to mitigate player advantage?
[2018-06-02 19:20] masn6811 (Masne98888): specifically p2 green sets, but also help in fastimus/nat A situations
[2018-06-02 19:21] masn6811 (Masne98888): often, fastimus is winning, and p1 can't go half a turn earlier, because elyot animus is so bad without a doomed drone or similar
[2018-06-02 19:21] masn6811 (Masne98888): i don't expect the pivot to help that much, but a forcefield is a forcefield
[2018-06-02 19:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): also, p1's base+fab line loses to p2's fastimus, and while this is fine with some units, i feel that if fab doesn't work there, it's generally harder to work in bigger sets
